sections.markdown:
# Fortinet FortiGate Firewall Log Analysis

This notebook contains DQL queries for analyzing Fortinet FortiOS syslog events ingested by the **Fortinet FortiGate Log Generator** Dynatrace workflow.

The workflow generates a realistic mix of traffic logs, IPS/UTM detections, VPN events, and system messages. Logs are ingested into Grail with structured `fortinet.*` attributes following the FortiOS 7.x key=value syslog format.

| Attribute | Values |
|---|---|
| `log.source` | `fortinet-fortigate` |
| `fortinet.type` | `traffic`, `utm`, `vpn`, `system`, `event` |
| `fortinet.action` | `accept`, `deny`, `server-rst`, `client-rst` |
| `fortinet.subtype` | `forward`, `ips`, `dns`, `ssl`, `local`, `generic` |

---

## 1. All Fortinet FortiGate Logs — Overview
All ingested log entries with key parsed fields. Use this as a starting point to confirm data is flowing.

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "fortinet-fortigate"
| fields timestamp,
         fortinet.type,
         fortinet.subtype,
         fortinet.action,
         fortinet.srcip,
         fortinet.dstip,
         fortinet.service,
         fortinet.policyname,
         fortinet.srcintf,
         fortinet.dstintf,
         fortinet.dstport,
         fortinet.sentbyte,
         fortinet.rcvdbyte,
         hostname
| sort timestamp desc
| limit 100

sections.markdown:
## 2. Blocked Traffic Only
All denied, reset, or blocked sessions from traffic logs. Validates the firewall **IS** blocking traffic.

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "fortinet-fortigate"
| filter fortinet.type == "traffic"
| filter fortinet.action != "accept"
| fields timestamp,
         fortinet.action,
         fortinet.srcip,
         fortinet.dstip,
         fortinet.service,
         fortinet.policyname,
         fortinet.srcintf,
         fortinet.dstintf,
         fortinet.dstport,
         fortinet.srccountry
| sort timestamp desc
| limit 100

sections.markdown:
## 3. Allowed / Accepted Traffic
All permitted sessions — confirms the firewall is **NOT** blocking everything.

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "fortinet-fortigate"
| filter fortinet.type == "traffic"
| filter fortinet.action == "accept"
| fields timestamp,
         fortinet.action,
         fortinet.srcip,
         fortinet.dstip,
         fortinet.service,
         fortinet.policyname,
         fortinet.dstport,
         fortinet.sentbyte,
         fortinet.rcvdbyte
| sort timestamp desc
| limit 100

sections.markdown:
## 4. Traffic Summary — Accept vs Block
Single-glance validation of the expected allow/deny mix.

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "fortinet-fortigate"
| filter fortinet.type == "traffic"
| fieldsAdd is_blocked = fortinet.action != "accept"
| summarize total = count(),
            accepted = countIf(NOT is_blocked),
            blocked = countIf(is_blocked)
| fieldsAdd accepted_pct = round(toDouble(accepted) / toDouble(total) * 100, decimals: 1),
            blocked_pct = round(toDouble(blocked) / toDouble(total) * 100, decimals: 1)

sections.markdown:
## 5. Blocked Traffic by Firewall Policy
Which deny policies are firing most? Useful for policy review and runbook creation.

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "fortinet-fortigate"
| filter fortinet.type == "traffic"
| filter fortinet.action != "accept"
| summarize block_count = count(),
            unique_sources = countDistinct(fortinet.srcip),
            unique_services = countDistinct(fortinet.service),
            by: { fortinet.policyname }
| sort block_count desc

sections.markdown:
## 6. Blocked Traffic by Service
Which services (protocols/ports) are being blocked most often?

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "fortinet-fortigate"
| filter fortinet.type == "traffic"
| filter fortinet.action != "accept"
| summarize block_count = count(),
            unique_sources = countDistinct(fortinet.srcip),
            policies_triggered = collectDistinct(fortinet.policyname),
            by: { fortinet.service }
| sort block_count desc

sections.markdown:
## 7. Blocked Traffic by Action Type
Breakdown of `deny` vs `server-rst` vs `client-rst` actions.

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "fortinet-fortigate"
| filter fortinet.type == "traffic"
| filter fortinet.action != "accept"
| summarize count = count(),
            by: { fortinet.action }
| sort count desc

sections.markdown:
## 8. Interface Pair Analysis
Traffic between physical/logical interfaces — reveals inbound vs outbound blocking patterns.

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "fortinet-fortigate"
| filter fortinet.type == "traffic"
| summarize total = count(),
            accepted = countIf(fortinet.action == "accept"),
            blocked = countIf(fortinet.action != "accept"),
            by: { fortinet.srcintf, fortinet.dstintf }
| fieldsAdd block_rate = round(toDouble(blocked) / toDouble(total) * 100, decimals: 1)
| sort block_rate desc

sections.markdown:
## 9. Top Blocked Destination IPs
Which internal IPs are receiving the most blocked traffic? Potential indicators of scan targets.

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "fortinet-fortigate"
| filter fortinet.type == "traffic"
| filter fortinet.action != "accept"
| summarize block_count = count(),
            attacking_sources = countDistinct(fortinet.srcip),
            services_targeted = collectDistinct(fortinet.service),
            by: { fortinet.dstip }
| sort block_count desc
| limit 20

sections.markdown:
## 10. Top Blocked Source IPs (with Country)
Which external source IPs are being blocked most? Includes source country for geo-context.

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "fortinet-fortigate"
| filter fortinet.type == "traffic"
| filter fortinet.action != "accept"
| summarize block_count = count(),
            targets = countDistinct(fortinet.dstip),
            policies_hit = collectDistinct(fortinet.policyname),
            country = takeFirst(fortinet.srccountry),
            by: { fortinet.srcip }
| sort block_count desc
| limit 20

sections.markdown:
## 11. Traffic Events Over Time
Time series of allowed vs blocked traffic — use to spot spikes, anomalies, or missing data.

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "fortinet-fortigate"
| filter fortinet.type == "traffic"
| fieldsAdd is_blocked = fortinet.action != "accept"
| makeTimeseries allowed = countIf(NOT is_blocked),
                 blocked = countIf(is_blocked),
                 interval: 5m

sections.markdown:
## 12. High-Risk Port Blocks
Blocks on sensitive ports: RDP (3389), SQL Server (1433), MySQL (3306), FTP (21), SMB (445), Telnet (23).

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "fortinet-fortigate"
| filter fortinet.action != "accept"
| filter fortinet.dstport == 3389
     OR fortinet.dstport == 1433
     OR fortinet.dstport == 3306
     OR fortinet.dstport == 21
     OR fortinet.dstport == 445
     OR fortinet.dstport == 23
| fields timestamp,
         fortinet.srcip,
         fortinet.dstip,
         fortinet.dstport,
         fortinet.service,
         fortinet.action,
         fortinet.policyname,
         fortinet.srccountry
| sort timestamp desc

sections.markdown:
## 13. IPS / UTM Detections
Intrusion prevention and unified threat management events. `fortinet.type == "utm"` with IPS detect actions. Includes attack name and severity.

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "fortinet-fortigate"
| filter fortinet.type == "utm"
| summarize count = count(),
            sources = countDistinct(fortinet.srcip),
            targets = countDistinct(fortinet.dstip),
            by: { fortinet.attack, fortinet.severity }
| sort count desc

sections.markdown:
## 14. Bandwidth Analysis by Service
Bytes sent and received for accepted connections, grouped by service. Blocked sessions transfer 0 bytes.

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "fortinet-fortigate"
| filter fortinet.type == "traffic"
| filter fortinet.action == "accept"
| summarize total_sent = sum(fortinet.sentbyte),
            total_received = sum(fortinet.rcvdbyte),
            session_count = count(),
            by: { fortinet.service }
| fieldsAdd total_bytes = total_sent + total_received
| sort total_bytes desc

sections.markdown:
## 15. Log Type Breakdown
All log entries grouped by `fortinet.type` (traffic, utm, vpn, system, event). Confirms the generator is producing the full expected log family mix.

input.value:
fetch logs
| filter log.source == "fortinet-fortigate"
| summarize count = count(),
            subtypes = collectDistinct(fortinet.subtype),
            by: { fortinet.type }
| sort count desc
